articles.title: Mira Murati’s deposition pulled back the curtain on Sam Altman’s ouster
articles.summary: Sam Altman's 2023 ouster from OpenAI is revealed through depositions in Musk v. Altman litigation. Testimony from former CTO Mira Murati provides new details about the dramatic board decision citing Altman's lack of candor.

articles.title: Apple’s AirPods with cameras for AI are apparently close to production
articles.summary: Apple's camera-equipped AirPods are progressing toward mass production, currently in design validation testing. The built-in cameras are intended for AI functionality rather than photo or video capture.

articles.title: SpaceX has a $55 billion plan to build AI chips in Texas
articles.summary: SpaceX is investing at least $55 billion to build an AI chip manufacturing plant called "Terafab" in Austin, Texas, with potential expansion to $119 billion.

articles.title: OpenAI launches new voice intelligence features in its API
articles.summary: OpenAI launched new voice intelligence features in its API with applications across customer service, education, and creator platforms.

articles.title: Voi founders’ new AI startup Pit has become the latest rising star out of Stockholm
articles.summary: Pit, a new AI startup founded by Voi co-founders, has raised $16 million in seed funding led by a16z, becoming Stockholm's latest rising tech star.

articles.title: OpenAI introduces new ‘Trusted Contact’ safeguard for cases of possible self-harm
articles.summary: OpenAI introduced a 'Trusted Contact' safeguard feature for ChatGPT users discussing self-harm. The tool expands the company's safety measures to protect vulnerable users during concerning conversations.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million Series B funding to build AI-native infrastructure challenging AWS. The investment capitalizes on developer demand for simpler, cost-effective alternatives to legacy cloud platforms.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code costs $20-$200/month for AI coding assistance, but free alternative Goose offers similar functionality as open-source software running locally without subscription fees.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M in Series B funding after a viral billboard hiring campaign featuring AI tokens and coding challenges. The creative recruitment stunt helped the startup attract top engineering talent in a competitive market.

articles.title: Mozilla says 271 vulnerabilities found by Mythos have "almost no false positives"
articles.summary: Mozilla's AI vulnerability detector Mythos found 271 Firefox security flaws with minimal false positives. The breakthrough demonstrates AI-assisted security tools are becoming practical for real-world vulnerability detection.

articles.title: Ars Asks: Share your shell and show us your tricked-out terminals!
articles.summary: Despite predictions that graphical interfaces would eliminate command lines, terminals remain essential tools for many tasks. The author discusses why text-based interfaces persist as superior to point-and-click alternatives.

articles.title: Widely used Daemon Tools disk app backdoored in monthlong supply-chain attack
articles.summary: Daemon Tools disk mounting software was backdoored for a month, with malicious updates distributed through official developer servers. Infected versions 12.5.0.2421-2434 on Windows collect system information and run malware at boot time.

articles.title: The Download: the tech reshaping IVF and the rise of balcony solar
articles.summary: New technologies including AI, robotics, and genetic editing are transforming IVF to make it faster, cheaper, and more effective, though raising ethical concerns.

articles.title: The balcony solar boom is coming to the US
articles.summary: US states are considering legislation to allow plug-in balcony solar systems that require minimal installation and could reduce emissions and power bills. While popular in Europe, safety concerns with existing home electrical equipment remain.

articles.title: What’s next for IVF
articles.summary: IVF has advanced significantly since the first test-tube baby 48 years ago, but challenges remain including high costs, low success rates, and accessibility issues.

articles.title: vLLM V0 to V1: Correctness Before Corrections in RL
articles.summary: vLLM advances from V0 to V1 by prioritizing correctness in reinforcement learning before making corrections. The update emphasizes accuracy foundations for more reliable AI model optimization.

articles.title: Adding Benchmaxxer Repellant to the Open ASR Leaderboard
articles.summary: The Open ASR Leaderboard introduces Benchmaxxer Repellant to prevent overfitting and gaming of rankings. This ensures fair evaluation of automatic speech recognition systems.

articles.title: Granite 4.1 LLMs: How They’re Built
articles.summary: Granite 4.1 LLMs represent advances in language model architecture and training techniques. The article explores the construction methods and design choices that enable improved performance.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI technology and its potential impact. The conversation explores upcoming developments and trends in the artificial intelligence field.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to boost productivity and creativity in content handling. The technology enables companies to work more efficiently while expanding imaginative capabilities.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft has open-sourced its 'farm of the future' toolkit, making agricultural AI technology publicly available to advance sustainable farming practices and improve crop yields.

articles.title: 5 gardening tips you can try right in Search
articles.summary: Google's AI Mode, Search Live, and Shopping features provide five gardening tips to help plants thrive. Users can leverage these tools for gardening advice and plant care information.

articles.title: Google is partnering with XPRIZE and Range Media Partners on the $3.5 million Future Vision film competition.
articles.summary: Google partners with XPRIZE and Range Media Partners to launch the Future Vision film competition with a $3.5 million prize pool.

articles.title: The latest AI news we announced in April 2026
articles.summary: Google announced its latest AI updates in April 2026, introducing new capabilities and improvements to its AI products and services.

articles.title: AlphaEvolve: How our Gemini-powered coding agent is scaling impact across fields
articles.summary: AlphaEvolve, powered by Gemini algorithms, scales AI impact across business, infrastructure, and science sectors. The coding agent demonstrates broad applications for solving complex problems.

articles.title: Enabling a new model for healthcare with AI co-clinician
articles.summary: Researchers are developing an AI co-clinician to augment healthcare delivery, exploring how AI can work alongside medical professionals to improve patient care outcomes.

articles.title: Announcing our partnership with the Republic of Korea
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with South Korea to accelerate scientific breakthroughs using frontier AI models, advancing research capabilities through collaborative innovation.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #340 - OpenAI vs Musk + Microsoft, DeepSeek v4, Vision Banana
articles.summary: Elon Musk testified in his lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman, claiming OpenAI misappropriated a charitable organization. Evidence revealed Musk's xAI used OpenAI models for training.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #243 - GPT 5.5, DeepSeek V4, AI safety sabotage
articles.summary: Episode discusses OpenAI's GPT-5.5 release with coding improvements and safety testing, xAI's Grok Voice Think Fast 1.0 for real-time voice agents, and concerns about AI safety sabotage.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #242 - ChatGPT Images 2.0, Qwen 3.6 Max, Kimi-K2.6
articles.summary: OpenAI released ChatGPT Images 2.0 with improved text and screenshot generation. The episode discusses recent AI advances including Qwen 3.6 Max and Kimi-K2.6 models.

articles.title: AI helping ease the UK’s NHS burden
articles.summary: AI-enabled virtual care is emerging as a solution to ease NHS burden by managing patients outside hospital settings, addressing waiting lists, hospital capacity, and corridor care amid staff shortages.

articles.title: HP and the art of AI and data for the enterprise
articles.summary: HP discusses leveraging enterprise data for AI, exploring challenges of data preparation and the choice between cloud-hosted versus local compute solutions for business AI implementation.

articles.title: US government increases AI suppliers and rethinks Anthropic’s role
articles.summary: The US Pentagon expanded its AI supplier roster to include Microsoft, Amazon, Nvidia, and Reflection AI for classified operations. Anthropic faced contract cancellation after refusing unrestricted military use terms.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, addressing debugging challenges in collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses localization, self-positioning, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: How Memory Sparse Attention scales LLM memory to 100 million tokens
articles.summary: Memory Sparse Attention (MSA) enables LLMs to handle context windows of 100 million tokens while maintaining accuracy, representing a significant breakthrough in scaling language model memory capacity.

articles.title: Claude Code is leaking API keys into public package registries
articles.summary: A study found that Claude Code and similar AI coding assistants are accidentally leaking sensitive API keys to public code repositories and package registries.

articles.title: Anthropic’s MCP vulnerability: When ‘expected behavior’ becomes a supply chain nightmare
articles.summary: Security researchers discovered a major architectural flaw in Anthropic's Model Context Protocol that exposes millions of AI applications to remote takeovers, revealing a critical supply chain vulnerability.

articles.title: After using Lenovo's $2,600 Yoga, I'm taking premium Windows laptops seriously again
articles.summary: Lenovo's $2,600 Yoga Pro 9i Aura Edition impresses with strong sustained performance, vivid display, and robust cooling, rekindling interest in premium Windows laptops.

articles.title: Roku apps loading slow? 9 quick fixes I try before blaming my Wi-Fi
articles.summary: Roku app slowness isn't always Wi-Fi related. The article provides quick troubleshooting fixes to improve performance before blaming your internet connection.

articles.title: Google Maps vs. Apple Maps: I compared two of the best navigation apps - here's my pick
articles.summary: Apple Maps has significantly improved and now rivals Google Maps. The article compares both navigation apps to determine which is superior for users today.

articles.title: The Roadmap to Mastering Tool Calling in AI Agents
articles.summary: The article outlines strategies for effectively implementing tool calling in AI agents, enabling them to interact with external systems and APIs to enhance their capabilities and perform complex tasks.

articles.title: Implementing Statistical Guardrails for Non-Deterministic Agents
articles.summary: The article discusses implementing statistical guardrails for non-deterministic agents, which produce varying outputs from identical inputs across multiple runs.

articles.title: Agentic RAG Explained in 3 Levels of Difficulty
articles.summary: Agentic RAG is explained through three difficulty levels, building understanding of this advanced AI approach that combines retrieval-augmented generation with autonomous agent capabilities for improved AI system performance.

articles.title: The Joy of Typing
articles.summary: A guide exploring modern type annotations in Python for data science applications, helping developers write more robust and maintainable code.

articles.title: Give Your AI Unlimited Updated Context
articles.summary: The article explores a portable knowledge layer architecture that enables AI systems to maintain unlimited, continuously updated context through automated mechanisms.

articles.title: How Major Reasoning Models Converge to the Same “Brain” as They Model Reality Increasingly Better
articles.summary: Advanced reasoning models converge toward similar architectures as they improve at modeling reality, suggesting one optimal approach exists for understanding the world.

articles.title: Powering the Next American Century: US Energy Secretary Chris Wright and NVIDIA’s Ian Buck on the Genesis Mission
articles.summary: U.S. Energy Secretary Chris Wright and NVIDIA's Ian Buck argue that AI leadership depends on energy leadership, with AI helping to generate the power it needs for advancement.

articles.title: Linked and Loaded: Gaijin Single Sign-On Now Available on GeForce NOW
articles.summary: GeForce NOW now supports Gaijin Single Sign-On, allowing faster logins for players accessing Gaijin games. Seven new games joined the cloud gaming service this week.

articles.title: NVIDIA Spectrum-X — the Open, AI-Native Ethernet Fabric — Sets the Standard for Gigascale AI, Now With MRC
articles.summary: NVIDIA Spectrum-X Ethernet is an advanced AI networking fabric enabling gigascale AI infrastructure, now featuring MRC technology. Deployed by OpenAI, Microsoft, and Oracle.

articles.title: Secure short-term GPU capacity for ML workloads with EC2 Capacity Blocks for ML and SageMaker training plans
articles.summary: AWS introduces EC2 Capacity Blocks for ML and SageMaker training plans to address GPU scarcity, enabling companies to secure reliable short-term GPU capacity for machine learning workloads amid high demand.

articles.title: Overcoming reward signal challenges: Verifiable rewards-based reinforcement learning with GRPO on SageMaker AI
articles.summary: AWS demonstrates reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) to address reward signal reliability challenges in LLM training. This approach improves model performance by introducing verification and transparency into feedback mechanisms for objective tasks.

articles.title: Agents that transact: Introducing Amazon Bedrock AgentCore payments, built with Coinbase and Stripe
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock introduces AgentCore payments with Coinbase and Stripe, enabling AI agents to autonomously transact and pay for resources in real-time during task execution.

articles.title: Achieving Peak System and Workload Efficiency on NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 with Slurm Block Scheduling
articles.summary: NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 extends NVLink coherence across entire racks for exascale performance, requiring new scheduling approaches like Slurm block scheduling to optimize rack-scale locality and system efficiency.

articles.title: Model Quantization: Post-Training Quantization Using NVIDIA Model Optimizer
articles.summary: Model quantization reduces VRAM usage and improves inference performance on NVIDIA GPUs by lowering computational requirements while maintaining model quality for resource-constrained environments.

articles.title: Real-Time Performance Monitoring and Faster Debugging with NCCL Inspector and Prometheus
articles.summary: NVIDIA introduces NCCL Inspector and Prometheus integration for real-time performance monitoring and debugging of GPU-to-GPU communication in distributed deep learning systems.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study examines how different generations perceive and interact with social media advertisements across various platforms, exploring generational differences in consumer attitudes toward ads.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta aims for net zero emissions by 2030 through developing carbon mitigation technology and sharing it openly with the public.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with a non-traditional background developed innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how unconventional paths can lead to breakthrough advances in emerging tech fields.

articles.title: Gradient-based Planning for World Models at Longer Horizons
articles.summary: GRASP is a gradient-based planner for world models enabling long-horizon planning through parallel trajectory optimization, stochastic exploration, and gradient reshaping for cleaner action signals.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers are developing methods to understand LLM behavior at scale through feature attribution and interpretability analysis, advancing safer and more transparent AI systems.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research presents information-driven design methodology for imaging systems, using encoders to map objects to images while quantifying measurement quality through noise models and information estimation techniques.
